physicalDetails.detail: Hard-weather harbor captain introduced in chapter20 as a woman built by sea labor and command presence, fully at home on a rough deck in ugly water.

Chapter 21 keeps Dagny briefly on the page at Daggercap Bay as a fur-lined harbor captain moving carefully over ice and wreckage, still reading as someone more concerned with survival windows than ceremony.

Chapter 22 brings Dagny back as pure deck authority: standing in the middle of the turnabout with both gloves tucked through her belt, reading Jaina in one captain’s glance, and later working the Wolfheart by the inch while every board in the ship shudders around her.

voiceMarkers.detail: Speaks in practical maritime authority: price, weather, ship handling, and hard limits stated without ornament or deference.
Her chapter-21 speech stays in the same hard harbor register: two days, maybe three, break ice if you want the ship to matter when you come back.

Her voice gets even harder under urgent return conditions: no blankets, no hammocks, short this time, not a corpse on my deck. She talks like a captain managing a dangerous engine she did not build but fully intends to survive.

beliefs.belief: A crossing this dangerous is still a job that can be done if the price is paid and the crew keeps its feet under real command.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter20 by the fact that she gets the Wolfheart across despite hostile headwinds.

beliefs.belief: A northern harbor window closes fast; if the pursuing party is not back on time, the bay will choose for them.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Active in chapter21 and supported by her immediate timing limit on the Wolfheart’s availability.

beliefs.belief: The return voyage can be shortened if the mage pays for it and the captain keeps absolute control over how that payment is spent.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter22 by the way Dagny tunes sail, food, timing, and demanded effort around Jaina’s rapidly worsening condition.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Get the Wolfheart back to Southshore fast without letting Jaina’s usefulness turn into a dead mage on the deck.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Deliver the pursuit to Lordaeron in time to matter while keeping ship and crew under sane command.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Unsparing, practical, and unfooled by heroics, with all sympathy translated into speed, food, and hard limits.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Accepts the northbound passage under dangerous conditions and takes the Wolfheart out anyway.
Keeps command of the ship's real movement even while Jaina's magic and the hostile headwinds threaten to turn the crossing into something unseamanlike.
Sets a hard waiting window at Daggercap Bay and makes clear that any return depends on crew help, broken ice, and time not yet spent.
Turns the Wolfheart around immediately at Daggercap Bay, spends every usable warning lane before clearing ice, and forbids any version of comfort that would cost them time.
Forces food, short-cast pacing, and deck discipline on Jaina throughout the crossing to keep ship-speed from becoming self-immolation.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Captains a hard sea crossing through supernatural resistance without surrendering authority over the vessel.
Reads ship, weather, and crew strain cleanly enough to keep everyone alive while the crossing gets worse.
Reads the bay as a closing logistical window rather than a neutral harbor and sets realistic survival terms for a delayed departure.
Can tune a ship’s movement around a mage’s intermittent power without surrendering seamanship to spectacle.
Reads exhaustion, rigging strain, and the real margin before death more accurately than anyone else on deck.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Narration and passengers
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Mostly as Captain Dagny Ironhull or Captain Ironhull, emphasizing role before intimacy.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Narration and crew
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Still treated as captain first, with role and competence more important than intimacy.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Uses the blunt practical register of a captain addressing cargo that must not become a problem under sail.
Mostly as the mage when speaking about effort and survivability, converting a person into the exact ship-function she needs without ceasing to notice the body inside it.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Reads as pure ship-command competence: planted stance, weather-balanced movement, and the kind of deck certainty that lets others borrow steadiness from her.
In chapter21 that confidence shifts from deck to ice: careful footing, no wasted sympathy, and a face already calculating weather and freeze before anyone has finished the conversation.
Plants herself at the center of hard deck labor, snaps orders without looking back for obedience, and measures Jaina in one flattening glance whenever the next push risks taking too much.

knowledge.detail: Understands the crossing's ordinary maritime danger and quickly recognizes when something more hostile than weather is working against the ship.
Knows Daggercap Bay and can bring the Wolfheart into it even after the voyage has turned punishing.
Knows the bay will close quickly enough that the inland party gets two good days, maybe three with help, before the ship’s waiting cost becomes madness.
Learns exactly how much speed can be bought from Jaina before the mage becomes more problem than engine, and adjusts the crossing around that moving limit.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Introduced in chapter20 as a story-canon sea captain whose competence expands the pursuit from road campaign into maritime chase.
Chapter 20 establishes Dagny as a hard practical captain who can keep a ship alive inside someone else's larger supernatural war.
Chapter 21 keeps Dagny in the same narrow but necessary role: not an inland participant, but the hard practical edge of whether this expedition has any return path at all.

arc.currentPosition: Chapter 22 keeps Dagny in her precise functional role and sharpens it: not just transport, but the captain who makes the return possible by refusing every romantic version of sacrifice that would sink ship or mission.

goalsHistory.active: Complete the Daggercap Bay run and put the passengers ashore alive.
goalsHistory.underlying: Hold authority over her ship in conditions that would kill a less disciplined captain or crew.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the Wolfheart ready at Daggercap Bay for as long as ice and weather allow.
goalsHistory.underlying: Remain the expedition’s exit strategy without sacrificing ship or crew to impossible waiting.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Hard-used, unsentimental, and professionally satisfied only in the narrow sense that the ship made landfall and the job got done.

Unsparing, practical, and on the clock. Landfall solved only the crossing; chapter21 leaves her holding the narrower problem of how long a sane captain waits in ice for soldiers chasing a prince inland.
